sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.24 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: We say that a group is sparse\/ if the variety generated by it has at most countably many subvarieties. Does there exist a finitely generated sparse group that has undecidable word problem? { A.\,V.\,Kuznetsov} Yes, such groups do exist; for example, a free group of${ N}_3{ A}$. By (A.\,N.\,Krasil'nikov, Math. USSR--Izv., { 37} (1991), 539--553) every subvariety of${ N}_3{ A}$has a finite basis fo...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
